headline: Goldman: AI PCs to buck 10% market slump as ‘edge computing’ demand accelerates
summary: Investing.com -- The global personal computer market is bracing for a significant contraction in 2026 as rising component costs and a cooling replacement cycle take their toll on shipments. According to a new report from Goldman Sachs, global PC shipments are now expected to decline by 10% year-on-year in 2026, a steeper drop than previously anticipated, as the industry grapples with the fallout from the Windows 10 "end-of-life" transition.

content.title: Goldman: AI PCs to buck 10% market slump as ‘edge computing’ demand accelerates

headline: Dell’s Doudna Supercomputer Role And What It May Mean For Investors
summary: Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) is partnering with the U.S. Department of Energy and NVIDIA on the new Doudna supercomputer. The system is designed to accelerate U.S. scientific discovery using AI and high performance computing. Doudna is expected to deliver more than ten times the performance of current leading systems. The project marks a new public private initiative for Dell beyond its commercial AI infrastructure work. For you as an investor, this project sits at the intersection of AI...

headline: Equinix (EQIX) Names Olivier Leonetti as Chief Financial Officer
summary: Equinix Inc. (NASDAQ:EQIX) is one of the best AI stocks to invest in according to billionaires. Leonetti, an experienced executive with over 30 years of financial leadership in the technology and infrastructure sectors, joins the digital infrastructure leader […]

headline: Morgan Stanley Raises Dell (DELL) PT to $110 Amid AI Growth, Margin Uncertainty
